patch.value:
## Context

File: src/core/lock/lock-gc.ts — contains full stale-lock classification logic (ttl_seconds threshold, unreadable, unparseable, invalid_timestamp). This logic is currently only exercised via `pm gc --scope locks`.

File: src/cli/commands/health.ts line 1471 — checks array is assembled here; no locks entry exists.

The remediation registry (src/core/diagnostics/remediation.ts) also has no `locks_*` warning codes.

## Acceptance criteria

- New health check name `locks` added alongside storage check.
- Reports: active_lock_count, stale_lock_count, unreadable_lock_count, unparseable_lock_count.
- Emits `locks_stale_count:<n>` warning when stale_lock_count > 0; emits `locks_unreadable:<n>` warning when unreadable > 0.
- Remediation registry entries: `locks_stale_count` → `pm gc --scope locks`, `locks_unreadable` → `pm gc --scope locks --dry-run`.
- Summarized in brief/summary health projection modes.
- 100% unit-test coverage on the new pure-logic path.

## Evidence

src/cli/commands/health.ts:1471 — checks assembled, no locks check.
src/core/lock/lock-gc.ts — classification logic is reusable.
Add pm health 'locks' check: surface stale item-claim counts before gc is needed

pm health runs 9 checks (settings, directories, settings_values, telemetry, extensions, storage, integrity, history_drift, vectorization) but has no check for the locks/ directory. Stale item-claim locks from crashed processes silently block agents from mutating items; the only existing remedy is `pm gc --scope locks` (an explicit mutation). A read-only health check exposing active-lock count, stale-lock count, and unparseable-lock count would let agents gate on a precondition rather than running gc speculatively.
pm health --json includes a 'locks' check; stale locks emit a warn-level warning with remediation_map pointing to pm gc --scope locks; no new required-check in brief mode unless stale > 0.

- New health check name `locks` added alongside storage check.
- Reports: active_lock_count, stale_lock_count, unreadable_lock_count, unparseable_lock_count.
- Emits `locks_stale_count:<n>` warning when stale_lock_count > 0; emits `locks_unreadable:<n>` warning when unreadable > 0.
- Remediation registry entries: `locks_stale_count` → `pm gc --scope locks`, `locks_unreadable` → `pm gc --scope locks --dry-run`.
- Summarized in brief/summary health projection modes.
- 100% unit-test coverage on the new pure-logic path.

value.author: history-health-agent
value.text: Implemented pm health locks check. Design: (1) lock classification extracted into pure classifyLockContent(file, raw, nowMs) in src/core/lock/lock-gc.ts (details: active | expired | unparseable_json | invalid_timestamp; the latter two roll up to the coarse 'unparseable' entry reason) — runLockGc now consumes it, so gc and health share ONE staleness/parse policy. (2) New read-only scanLockHealth(pmRoot, now?) walks locks/*.lock and counts active/stale/unreadable/unparseable; never removes anything; ghost files (ENOENT between readdir and readFile) are skipped; missing locks dir = all-zero scan. (3) health.ts buildLocksCheck inserts the 'locks' check right after storage; warnings locks_stale_count:<n> / locks_unreadable:<n>; status warn when either fires. (4) Remediation registry: locks_stale_count -> 'pm gc --scope locks', locks_unreadable -> 'pm gc --scope locks --dry-run' (disjoint codes, first-match resolver stays 100%-coverable; generic mutual-exclusivity guard in remediation-registry.spec covers them). (5) Projection: counts appear in default/full and brief (remediation_map stripped in brief); summary mode lists the check with empty details like every other check; locks is never 'skipped' so it always projects.

patch.value: pm health had no visibility into locks/; stale item-claim locks from crashed processes silently blocked mutations and agents had to run pm gc --scope locks speculatively.

New 'locks' check sits right after storage in every projection mode (counts in default/full/brief, listed in summary); pure classifyLockContent extraction means gc and health share one staleness/parse policy; scanLockHealth never mutates; warnings locks_stale_count:<n> and locks_unreadable:<n> map to 'pm gc --scope locks' and 'pm gc --scope locks --dry-run' in the remediation registry and the check's remediation_map. New pure paths fully unit-tested (classifyLockContent all four outcomes incl. ttl boundary; scanLockHealth missing-dir/mixed/unreadable/ghost/readdir-error).

Shipped read-only pm health locks check: active/stale/unreadable/unparseable lock counts via the same classification pm gc --scope locks acts on, with locks_stale_count/locks_unreadable warnings and remediation entries.
